rationale: Flagged high on the report; fasting reference per sheet is 85-99 mg/dL.

text: Glucose is mildly elevated above the fasting reference range, consistent with reduced insulin sensitivity or recent dietary/stress influence. Recommend correlating with HbA1c, triglycerides, and CRP; consider confirming fasting status of the draw.
rationale: Synthesized from the Internal Notes column for Glucose, which flags severity weight 3/5 and lists possible influences and marker pairings.

rationale: Same flagged report line, carried into the patient-facing section.

text: Glucose is the sugar your body uses for energy. Your result was a little above the typical range, which can reflect diet, stress, or timing of the test. Keeping it in a steady range is part of general metabolic wellness.
rationale: Patient-friendly synthesis drawn from the Explanation column for Glucose.

text: Build meals around fiber-rich foods like vegetables, beans, and whole grains. Ease back on sugary drinks and refined snacks. Take a 10-15 minute walk after your largest meals. Aim for consistent, adequate sleep.
rationale: Taken directly from the 30-Day Plan column for Glucose.

text: Keep a regular pattern of balanced meals and daily movement, and revisit this marker periodically (for example, every few months) to watch your trend. Share your results with your healthcare provider.
